mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding supply capacity and economic momentum, which historically supports positive equity returns as corporate earnings grow.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over 2-year rolling window or INDPRO declines while S&P 500 returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 700,000 units, it signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence, historically leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In backtests, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST > 700 (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate synchronized demand-supply expansion, a powerful signal of economic strength that boosts equity valuations.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO rise month-on-month, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases or Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate environment, the Federal Reserve's accommodative stance compresses discount rates and encourages equity risk-taking, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.50%, the S&P 500 20-day return has a negative median or Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady nonfarm payroll growth reflects hiring momentum and labor market health, which historically drives consumer spending and corporate earnings, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases sequentially, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of quarters or IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP exceeds $13.5 trillion, it signals the economy operating above trend, which often precedes mean-reversion but in early cycles can extend gains. In the context of the 2017 recovery, crossing that level is positive for equities.
failure_condition: In periods when GDPC1 is above 13500, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative more often than positive (sign test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high unemployment and ultra-low interest rates indicates extreme monetary accommodation, which historically drives risk-on behavior and positive equity returns as the central bank supports growth.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5% and FEDFUNDS < 2%, S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-driven recovery, rising industrial production with stable consumer prices signals real economic growth unaccompanied by overheating, which is particularly supportive for short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During supply recovery regimes (identifiable by stable CPIAUCSL and rising INDPRO), the S&P 500 5-day return has Rank IC below 0.04 over 2 years.
